free_text: Cross-section measurements of (n,2n) and (n,p)
reactions on 124,126,128,130,131,132Xe in the 14 MeV
region and theoretical calculations of their
excitation functions
National Institute of Metrology, Beijing
National Institute for Radiological
Protection, China CDC, Beijing
K-400 Neutron Generator
The neutron yields were about (4-5)E10 n/s. The
14 MeV neutrons were produced by a 134 keV deuteron
beam energy and a 200 uA beam current. The generator
uses a solid 3H-Ti target that is 2.65 mg/cm2 thick.
Xenon gas (99.999% purity with natural isotopic
composition) was stored in a stainless-steel sphere
with a 20 mm inner diameter and 1 mm wall thickness.
The gas weight and pressure were 2.3-5.2 g and
120-250 atm. Five of such samples of xenon gas and 10
monitors (20 mm diameter), comprising 99.99% pure
and 0.12 mm thick Nb foil and a 99.999% pure and 0.3
mm thick Al foil, were prepared for irradiation.
The samples were irradiated for 2 h. A ZrNbAl-
Xe-AlNbZr stack was mounted at 0, 45, 90, 110, and
135 deg angles relative to the beam. The end of the
neutron production target was 5 cm apart from the
sample sphere center. The samples decay for different
amounts of time (ranging from 55 min to 5 days
depending on the half-life of the daughter nucleus).
The gamma-ray activities of the 123Xe, 125Xe,
127Xe, 129mXe, 131mXe, 130I, 131I, 24Na, 92mNb, and
132I nuclei were measured by the calibrated detector
of GEM-60P HPGe (with the crystal length and diameter
equal to 72.3 and 70.1 mm, respectively). The energy
resolution at 1.332 MeV and a relative efficiency
were equal to 1.69 keV and about 68%. The detector was
calibrated using standard sources of gamma-ray.
To monitor the neutron flux within the
irradiation, the accompanying alpha-particles were
utilized. An Au-Si surface barrier detector was set
at an angle of 135 deg relative to the beam
direction. The obtained data were adopted for the
correction of the neutron flux deviation.
F = fc*fs*fg*fo. fc, fs, fg, and fo denote the
correcting factors repres-enting the coincidence
summing impact of the cascade gamma-rays created by
the nuclides, the self-absorption of the gamma-rays,
the samples counting geometries, and the solid angle
of neutron flux. fs are distributed between 1.072 and
2.014. The value of the correction factor fo is 1.0756.
Correction factors for the self-absorption of the
sample at a given gamma-ray energy. The angles of the
five samples relative to the beam are 0 deg (No.1),
